name: home-assistant-blade-mcp
title: Home Assistant
description: Home Assistant operations — entities, state, history, device control, automations, energy, multi-site management
tagline: Multi-site Home Assistant control with write gating
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Entities & state — query any entity, filter by domain, area, or floor
History & energy — state history, energy monitoring, long-term statistics
Device control — call services, activate scenes, trigger automations
Automations — list, toggle, create, and manage automation rules
Multi-site — manage multiple HA instances from a single plugin
Write-gated — reads always work, device commands require explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Home Assistant Blade gives your AI agent full operational control over one or more Home Assistant instances. 36 tools cover entity state queries, device control, automation management, energy monitoring, history analysis, and scene activation.

Your agent can check which lights are on, review energy consumption for the past week, trigger an automation, activate a scene, and manage smart home devices across multiple sites — all in a single conversation.

### Multi-site support

Unlike most Home Assistant integrations, this plugin supports multiple instances natively. Set `HA_PROVIDERS` to a comma-separated list of site names, then configure per-provider URL and token environment variables. Each tool call is scoped to a specific provider, so your agent can compare energy usage between two homes or check device status across sites.

### Token efficiency

Home Assistant's REST API returns verbose entity state payloads with deep attribute nesting. Each tool includes a concise formatter that extracts only the fields your agent needs. Entity lists are filtered by domain, area, or floor so you never waste context on irrelevant devices.

### Safety model

All 36 tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — entity state, history, energy stats, automation list
- **Writes** require `HA_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — call services, activate scenes, toggle automations, create automations

This means your agent can monitor your home freely but cannot change anything without explicit permission.

tools.name: ha_info
tools.description: Health check — instance URL, version, entity count, area count.

tools.name: ha_config
tools.description: HA server configuration — components loaded, location, timezone.

tools.name: ha_error_log
tools.description: Recent error log entries from the HA instance.

tools.name: ha_areas
tools.description: List all areas with entity counts.

tools.name: ha_devices
tools.description: List all devices with manufacturer, model, area assignment.

tools.name: ha_entities
tools.description: List entities, optionally filtered by domain, area, or label.

tools.name: ha_floors
tools.description: List all floors with area assignments.

tools.name: ha_labels
tools.description: List all labels with entity counts.

tools.name: ha_search
tools.description: Fuzzy search across entities, devices, areas, and automations.

tools.name: ha_services_list
tools.description: List all available services across loaded components.

tools.name: ha_state
tools.description: Current state and attributes of a single entity by entity_id.

tools.name: ha_states
tools.description: Current state of all entities — bulk state snapshot.

tools.name: ha_states_by_domain
tools.description: Current state of all entities within a domain (light, switch, sensor, etc.).

tools.name: ha_history
tools.description: State history for one or more entities in a time range.

tools.name: ha_logbook
tools.description: Logbook entries (state changes + automation triggers) for a time range.

tools.name: ha_statistics
tools.description: Long-term statistics for entities (hourly/daily aggregates).

tools.name: ha_statistics_list
tools.description: List all entities with long-term statistics available.

tools.name: ha_calendar_events
tools.description: Events from HA calendar entities in a date range.

tools.name: ha_call_service
tools.description: Call an arbitrary HA service with a data payload. Write.

tools.name: ha_light
tools.description: Control a light entity — on/off, brightness, colour. Write.

tools.name: ha_climate
tools.description: Control a climate entity — mode, setpoint, fan, swing. Write.

tools.name: ha_scene
tools.description: Activate a scene by entity_id. Write.

tools.name: ha_lock
tools.description: Lock or unlock a lock entity. Write — destructive.

tools.name: ha_alarm
tools.description: Arm, disarm, or trigger an alarm control panel. Write — destructive.

tools.name: ha_automations
tools.description: List all automation entities with state and last triggered.

tools.name: ha_automation_get
tools.description: Get full configuration (triggers, conditions, actions) for an automation.

tools.name: ha_automation_trigger
tools.description: Force-trigger an automation. Write.

tools.name: ha_automation_toggle
tools.description: Enable or disable an automation. Reversible write.

tools.name: ha_automation_create
tools.description: Create a new automation from a YAML/JSON spec. Write.

tools.name: ha_automation_delete
tools.description: Delete an automation by ID. Destructive.

tools.name: ha_script_run
tools.description: Execute a script entity by entity_id. Write.

tools.name: ha_template
tools.description: Render a Jinja2 template against current HA state. Read.

tools.name: ha_camera_snapshot
tools.description: Capture a still image from a camera entity.

tools.name: ha_webhook
tools.description: Trigger a webhook automation by webhook ID. Write.

tools.name: ha_events
tools.description: Recent state-change events from HA event bus.

tools.name: ha_notify
tools.description: Send a notification via an HA notify service. Write.

env.name: HA_PROVIDERS
env.description: Comma-separated provider names for multi-instance mode (e.g. sandybay,paddington)

setup.blurb: Connect to Home Assistant for smart home control, monitoring, and automation management. Supports multi-site.
setup.help_text: Create a Long-Lived Access Token at Profile → Security → Long-Lived Access Tokens in your Home Assistant instance. For multi-site, set HA_PROVIDERS and per-provider URL/token env vars.

fields.label: Home Assistant URL
fields.help: The base URL of your Home Assistant instance

fields.label: Enable Write Operations
fields.help: Set to 'true' to enable device control, scene activation, and automation triggers

scenarios.label: Evening shutdown routine
scenarios.body: Check which lights and devices are still on across the house, turn off non-essential items, arm the security system, and confirm the final state.

scenarios.label: Weekly energy audit
scenarios.body: Pull energy consumption data for the past week, compare usage across areas, identify the highest-consuming devices, and suggest automation rules to reduce waste.

scenarios.label: Cross-site status check
scenarios.body: Query entity states across multiple Home Assistant instances, compare climate readings between sites, and flag any offline devices or triggered alarms.
